rules.description: Documentation indicator for international shipment customs processing, such as INV, EDI-DOC, or KEY.
Numeric code determining the ordering of postal code, city, and state within the consignee address line.
Single-character flag within MaxiCode data indicating whether the delivery address was validated against a CASS-certified database.
Indicates the billing method for the shipment, such as P/P for prepaid.
Single-digit check digit at the end of the UPS tracking number calculated using the Modified MOD 10 algorithm.
City name within the consignee address block, positioned according to the destination country's address format.
Three-digit numeric code identifying the UPS class of service, used internally for service identification.
The full destination address block for the package recipient including street, city, state/province, postal code, and country.
Brief text description of the package contents for customs and shipment identification purposes.
ISO 3166 alpha-2 country code for the destination country, used on the label and within MaxiCode data.
Full name of the destination country printed as the last line of the consignee address block on international shipments.
ISO 3166 three-digit numeric country code for the destination, encoded within the MaxiCode data string.
Solid black lines (0.1 inch thick) located immediately above and below the UPS barcode block to visually delimit the barcode area.
Human-readable text printed below or adjacent to each barcode symbol, exactly matching the encoded barcode data.
Three-digit Julian day of the year (001-366) representing the pickup date, encoded in the MaxiCode data string.
Version identifier of the UPS label specification used to generate the label, such as 18.5A 01/2020.
Two-dimensional MaxiCode barcode encoding destination postal code, country code, class of service, tracking number, and address for automated sortation.
Three-digit numeric code representing the UPS service level encoded in the MaxiCode primary message, derived from the 1Z service level indicator.
MaxiCode encoding mode where Mode 2 is used for U.S. destinations and Mode 3 for all non-U.S. destinations.
Postal code encoded within MaxiCode data string: Mode 2 (U.S.) 5 or 9 numeric digits, Mode 3 (non-U.S.) up to 6 alphanumeric characters
Consignee street address encoded in MaxiCode secondary message, 1-35 uppercase characters or primary/secondary format separated by FS character
Destination city name encoded in MaxiCode secondary message, 1-20 uppercase alphanumeric characters
3-digit ISO numeric country code for the destination country encoded in MaxiCode primary message
Destination postal code encoded in MaxiCode primary message, up to 9 numeric digits (Mode 2) or 6 alphanumeric characters (Mode 3)
2-character state or province code for the destination encoded in MaxiCode secondary message
ISO 3166 alpha-2 country code for the shipment origin country, located in the ship-from address block
Human-readable package count displayed as 'X OF Y' indicating current package number and total packages in shipment
Package weight displayed as human-readable text prefixed with 'SHP WT:', rounded up to next whole pound or kilogram
Code 128 barcode encoding destination postal code with GS1 Application Identifier 420 (domestic) or 421 (international)
Destination postal/ZIP code in the ship-to address block, format and length vary by destination country
The unique 18-character UPS tracking number prefixed with 1Z, serving as the primary package identifier.
Customer-generated 7-character reference number embedded within the tracking number at positions 11-17
UPS routing code identifying destination sort facility, composed of country/state code, postal prefix, and sort code
3-position graphical service indicator showing class of service, time of day, and day of delivery
Two-character service level code embedded at positions 9-10 of the 1Z tracking number
UPS service level name printed prominently on the label identifying the shipping service
Date the shipment is tendered to UPS, displayed in DD MMM YYYY format prefixed with 'DATE:'
Origin/shipper address block containing contact name, phone number, company name, address lines, postal code line, and country.
Destination/receiver address block containing contact name, phone number, company name, address lines, postal code line, and country.
Narrow horizontal lines used to segregate barcode and information blocks on the label
Contact name for the shipper/sender, displayed as the first line of the ship-from address block.
Phone number at the origin/shipper location, required for international shipments except when shipping to/from the EU.
Company name of the shipper/sender displayed in the ship-from address block.
Optional additional address line for the shipper such as suite, floor, or building name.
Street address of the origin/shipper location, printed directly above the postal code line.
Postal/ZIP code and city line for the shipper address, format varies by country.
Country name of the shipper address, required for international shipments where origin and destination countries differ.
Text indicator 'SHIP TO:' printed to the left of the destination address block to identify the consignee address.
Contact name of the consignee/recipient displayed in the ship-to address block.
Telephone number of the contact at the destination address.
Company name at the destination/consignee address displayed in the ship-to address block.
Optional additional address line for the consignee such as suite, floor, or building name.
Postal/ZIP code and city line for the consignee address, format varies by destination country.
Full country name of the destination/consignee address, required for international shipments.
The street address of the destination/consignee displayed in the TO address block on the label.
The date the shipment is tendered, required for international movements, printed in DD MMM YYYY format with DATE: prefix.
The dimensional weight of the shipment, printed when applicable, rounded to next whole pound or half kilogram.
An optional 1-30 character alphanumeric shipment identifier encoded within the MaxiCode barcode data string.
The UPS shipment number calculated from the first package's tracking number, required for international shipments.
The total weight of the entire shipment (all packages combined), displayed with KG or LBS unit.
The UPS shipper identification number printed in the Package Information Block, prefixed with SHP# or SHP #.
The state, province, or county within the consignee address block, required for countries that use state/province designations.
Code 128 barcode encoding the full 18-character 1Z tracking number, the primary machine-readable barcode on the label.
The human-readable formatted tracking number printed below the UPS Service Title with specific spacing groups.
The 6-character alphanumeric UPS shipper account number encoded in the MaxiCode data string and embedded in the 1Z tracking number.
The UPS Routing Code used for package sorting, derived from the monthly URC data file, displayed next to the MaxiCode.
The Standard Carrier Alpha Code identifying UPS, always the fixed 4-character value UPSN encoded in the MaxiCode data string.
The complete UPS service name printed in uppercase bold letters beneath the top highlight bar in the Tracking Number Barcode Block.
The version number of the UPS Routing Code table used, printed in small font at the bottom of the carrier segment.

additional_routing_instructions.description: Documentation indicator for international shipment customs processing, such as INV, EDI-DOC, or KEY.

ship_to_address.description: Destination/receiver address block containing contact name, phone number, company name, address lines, postal code line, and country.

ship_to_country.description: Full country name of the destination/consignee address, required for international shipments.

ship_to_street_address.description: The street address of the destination/consignee displayed in the TO address block on the label.

shipment_date.description: The date the shipment is tendered, required for international movements, printed in DD MMM YYYY format with DATE: prefix.

shipment_dimensional_weight.description: The dimensional weight of the shipment, printed when applicable, rounded to next whole pound or half kilogram.

shipment_id_number.description: An optional 1-30 character alphanumeric shipment identifier encoded within the MaxiCode barcode data string.

shipment_number.description: The UPS shipment number calculated from the first package's tracking number, required for international shipments.

shipment_weight.description: The total weight of the entire shipment (all packages combined), displayed with KG or LBS unit.

shipper_number.description: The UPS shipper identification number printed in the Package Information Block, prefixed with SHP# or SHP #.

state_province.description: The state, province, or county within the consignee address block, required for countries that use state/province designations.

tracking_number_barcode.description: Code 128 barcode encoding the full 18-character 1Z tracking number, the primary machine-readable barcode on the label.

tracking_number_human_readable.description: The human-readable formatted tracking number printed below the UPS Service Title with specific spacing groups.

ups_account_number.description: The 6-character alphanumeric UPS shipper account number encoded in the MaxiCode data string and embedded in the 1Z tracking number.

ups_routing_code.description: The UPS Routing Code used for package sorting, derived from the monthly URC data file, displayed next to the MaxiCode.

ups_scac.description: The Standard Carrier Alpha Code identifying UPS, always the fixed 4-character value UPSN encoded in the MaxiCode data string.

urc_version_number.description: The version number of the UPS Routing Code table used, printed in small font at the bottom of the carrier segment.
